question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user flood GET /api/v1/topics/{id} with high-frequency concurrent requests using valid topic IDs, causing the HikariCP database connection pool shared with the importer to be fully exhausted, so the importer can no longer write new consensus transactions to the database, resulting in total network transaction confirmation shutdown?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user send thousands of simultaneous requests to GET /api/v1/topics/{id} with non-existent topic IDs, causing TopicServiceImpl to throw EntityNotFoundException on every call while holding open JPA EntityManager sessions, exhausting the shared database connection pool and blocking the importer from persisting new record files?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicRepository.findById() triggers three sequential database queries (topicService, entityService, customFeeService) per request in TopicController.getTopic(), multiplying database load by 3x per request, and use this amplification to exhaust database connections faster than a single-query endpoint, causing the importer to stall on transaction writes?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user craft requests to GET /api/v1/topics/{id} with IDs near Long.MAX_VALUE (e.g., 9223372036854775806), causing the CrudRepository<Topic, Long> findById() to execute a full table scan or index miss on the topic table, generating long-running queries that hold database locks and prevent the importer from acquiring write locks needed to confirm new transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly call GET /api/v1/topics/{id} with valid topic IDs that have large adminKey, feeExemptKeyList, feeScheduleKey, and submitKey byte arrays, causing Hibernate to deserialize large binary blobs on every request, consuming excessive heap memory and triggering JVM garbage collection pauses that freeze the rest-java service and indirectly starve the importer of shared infrastructure resources?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of rate limiting on GET /api/v1/topics/{id} to send a sustained flood of requests, causing the Spring thread pool handling HTTP requests to be fully occupied with TopicRepository.findById() calls, starving the importer's internal HTTP or gRPC callbacks and preventing new transaction records from being processed?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated EntityNotFoundException from TopicServiceImpl.findById() by querying non-existent IDs at high rate, causing excessive exception object allocation and stack trace generation in the JVM, leading to heap exhaustion (OutOfMemoryError) in the rest-java service that crashes the process and takes down dependent transaction confirmation pipelines?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to GET /api/v1/topics/{id} with IDs that map to topics having a timestampRange with an unbounded upper end (Range.atLeast()), causing Hibernate's Range type deserialization to perform expensive computation, and by flooding such requests exhaust CPU resources shared with the importer, preventing timely transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CrudRepository<Topic, Long> findById() method's reliance on Hibernate's first-level cache (EntityManager session cache) by sending requests that cause cache thrashing — repeatedly loading and evicting Topic entities — consuming excessive memory and CPU in the JPA layer, degrading the shared database to the point where the importer cannot write new transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user send concurrent requests to GET /api/v1/topics/{id} targeting the same valid topic ID, causing multiple simultaneous Hibernate sessions to each hold a shared read lock on the same topic row, and if the database uses row-level locking, prevent the importer's upsert operations on the topic table from acquiring write locks, stalling transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicRepository extends CrudRepository which exposes findAll() via the Spring Data infrastructure, and if any misconfiguration or framework bug exposes this endpoint, trigger a full table scan of the topic table, returning all topic records and exhausting database I/O bandwidth needed by the importer?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of pagination on GET /api/v1/topics/{id} combined with the CrudRepository<Topic, Long> findById() returning a full Topic entity including all binary fields (adminKey, feeExemptKeyList, feeScheduleKey, submitKey), to generate large HTTP responses that saturate network bandwidth and prevent the importer's outbound connections from functioning?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to GET /api/v1/topics/{id} with IDs that trigger Hibernate's second-level cache eviction storms — by alternating between cached and non-cached IDs — causing excessive cache lock contention in the shared JVM cache, degrading performance of all repository operations including those used by the importer?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the synchronous blocking nature of TopicRepository.findById() (JPA/JDBC) in a reactive-adjacent service, causing thread blocking that exhausts the bounded thread pool, preventing other critical service operations from executing and causing the rest-java service to stop processing importer health-check or callback requests?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to GET /api/v1/topics/{id} with IDs that map to topics with a createdTimestamp of 0 or negative values, causing unexpected behavior in Hibernate's entity mapping or the TopicMapper, throwing uncaught exceptions that crash the rest-java service and prevent transaction confirmation status queries?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that CrudRepository<Topic, Long> uses Long as the ID type, and send requests with ID value 0 (zero), causing the repository to execute a SELECT WHERE id=0 query that may behave unexpectedly in PostgreSQL (e.g., returning unexpected rows or causing a sequential scan), contributing to database load that blocks the importer?
